channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Judge Temporarily Blocks Hegseth From Punishing Mark Kelly Over ‘Illegal Orders’ Video
item.description: Judge Richard J. Leon found that attempts to discipline Mark Kelly for a video that warned against following illegal orders would violate the senator’...

item.title: The tech billionaires aren’t just grabbing trophy Florida mansions—they have competing half-billion-dollar megayachts jostling for dock space
item.description: $500,000 annual docking fees and private marinas are all part of the package for Florida’s wealthy seagoers.

item.title: The affordability crisis is driving unprecedented price cuts in the housing market, Realtor.com says
item.description: “Builders are clearly responding to today’s affordability pressures,” said Realtor.com chief economist Danielle Hale.

item.title: Europe Worries Trump Poses Threat to Its Financial and Tech Sovereignty
item.description: European leaders have been compelled to address the possibility of once-remote risks to the financial networks and technology that undergird their eco...

item.title: Instagram boss reveals he’s paid $900k per year plus stock worth ‘tens of millions of dollars’ as he denies ‘addiction’ claims
item.description: The plaintiff’s lawyer in a landmark social media addiction trial tried to connect Adam Mosseri’s compensation with Instagram’s policy toward filters.

item.title: Guard Troops Fully Withdraw From Chicago, Portland and Los Angeles
item.description: President Trump initially deployed the troops in those cities to support law enforcement efforts to tamp down protests against immigration raids and p...

item.title: One of Wall Street’s most feared hedge fund managers on the decline of the dollar: gold is ‘becoming the reserve asset’
item.description: Greenlight Capital's David Einhorn, known for his “Einhorn Effect” and years of touting gold, says the world's central banks don't love dollars anymor...

item.title: California Launches Civil Rights Investigation Into L.A. Eaton Fire Response
item.description: The state’s attorney general will examine whether emergency responders failed to adequately warn the residents of West Altadena, a Black middle-class ...

item.title: Trump Repeals Key Greenhouse Gas Finding, Erasing EPA’s Power to Fight Climate Change
item.description: The Environmental Protection Agency repealed the bedrock scientific finding that greenhouse gases threaten human life and well being. It means the age...

item.title: OpenClaw is the bad boy of AI agents. Here’s why security experts say you should beware
item.description: The open-source AI agent pushes autonomy to the edge — thrilling hobbyists and unnerving security experts

item.title: Europe Worries Trump Poses Threat to Its Financial and Tech Sovereignty
item.description: European leaders have been compelled to address the possibility of once-remote risks to the financial networks and technology that undergird their eco...

item.title: Israeli Army Reservists Are Suspected of Using Inside Knowledge to Bet
item.description: The authorities said several reservists and a civilian were arrested, and two were charged, after an investigation into the use of classified informat...

item.title: Europe’s Leaders Gather At Munich Summit, Reeling From Trump’s Criticism
item.description: Officials gather on Friday for Europe’s biggest annual security summit, where a speech by Vice President JD Vance last year started an unraveling of t...

item.title: Chloe Kim, once a teenage phenom, loses to a new one.
item.description: Kim was upset in heavy snow by a new star, 17-year-old, Choi Gaon of South Korea, in the women’s halfpipe.

item.title: El Niño May Be Back This Summer, Bringing Drought and Floods
item.description: The powerful weather pattern is expected to shift into gear again around June, NOAA said, though its strength this time remains a question.
